post_content:
A Concern of The Ibn Sina Trust

Established on April 10, 2009, Ibn Sina Hospital Sylhet Ltd. has been at the forefront of delivering advanced medical care, keeping pace with the continuous evolution of healthcare technologies. The hospital offers comprehensive tertiary care services across a range of specialties, including Internal Medicine (covering Neurology, Nephrology, Gastroenterology & Liver, Cardiology, Endocrinology), General Surgery, Gynecology, Neurosurgery, Spine Surgery, Colorectal Surgery, Orthopedic Surgery, and Urology.

Ibn Sina Hospital has also pioneered in Laparoscopic Abdominal and Colorectal Surgery, setting new standards in minimally invasive procedures. Its dedicated Dialysis Unit, equipped with state-of-the-art technology and supported by a skilled medical team, continues to provide affordable and high-quality care to an ever-growing number of kidney patients.

The hospital takes pride in its Intensive Care Unit (ICU), which operates 24/7 to deliver critical care through cutting-edge equipment and a team of experienced doctors, nurses, and healthcare professionals.

With four fully equipped operation theaters, including C-Arm and Operating Microscope facilities, Ibn Sina Hospital ensures precision and efficiency in surgical procedures—enhancing outcomes and ensuring patient safety.

post_excerpt: Ad-din Foundation was founded in 1980 with a mission to serve the underprivileged population of Bangladesh. Its journey began with the establishment of an orphanage in Jessore. Over the past 37 years, Ad-din has grown significantly, introducing a wide range…
post_content:
Ad-din Foundation was founded in 1980 with a mission to serve the underprivileged population of Bangladesh. Its journey began with the establishment of an orphanage in Jessore. Over the past 37 years, Ad-din has grown significantly, introducing a wide range of projects across different regions of the country.

Today, Ad-din operates hospitals in Dhaka, Keranigonj, Jessore, Kushtia, Khulna, and Postogola. It has also established medical colleges in Dhaka, Jessore, Keranigonj, and Khulna; a nursing college in Dhaka; nursing institutes in Dhaka, Jessore, and Kushtia; as well as a medical technology institute in Jessore. Additional initiatives include mobile eye clinics, maternal and child health programs, obstetric fistula prevention and repair services, and integrated health and microfinance programs with health insurance coverage.

The foundation’s hospitals were created to deliver high-quality healthcare at an affordable cost. Today, Ad-din hospitals are widely recognized for their excellence, affordability, and patient-centered approach.

The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare of Bangladesh has approved several Ad-din medical colleges: Ad-din Women’s Medical College (AWMC) in Dhaka in 2008, Ad-din Sakina Medical College (ASMC) in Jessore in 2012, Bashundhara Ad-din Medical College (BAMC) in Keranigonj in 2013, and Ad-din Akij Medical College (AAMC) in Khulna in 2013.

Ad-din is committed to ensuring high-quality teaching facilities and recruiting experienced faculty members, many of whom are recognized experts in their fields. With its available resources, the organization strives to produce competent medical graduates who excel in clinical practice while remaining mindful of their social responsibilities.

post_content:
Hypertension & Research Centre, Rangpur (HTN&RCR) is a sister concern of Dr. Wasim-Waleda Bahumukhi Kallayan Foundation, founded by Professor Dr. Md. Zakir Hossain, a distinguished medical professional with notable credentials. Established on November 14, 2008, the center focuses on the research, prevention, and treatment of Hypertension and Diabetes Mellitus, primarily through social mobilization and awareness-building activities.

Vision and Mission:

  • Vision: To improve the health and well-being of populations by eradicating preventable tragedies associated with non-communicable diseases (NCDs) such as Hypertension and Diabetes, ensuring that no one remains untreated.
  • Mission: To conduct research, provide affordable healthcare services, and promote awareness of NCDs through community-based efforts, aiming to reduce morbidity, mortality, and the negative effects of these diseases.

Objectives:

  • Popularize HTN&RCR as a unique and desired service center.
  • Reduce the major risk factors of cardiovascular diseases, particularly Hypertension and Diabetes.
  • Foster healthcare-seeking behavior through social mobilization and awareness-building activities.
  • Provide cost-free detection and affordable treatment for NCD patients.

Justification for Establishment:
Hypertension, a major public health problem affecting millions worldwide, is particularly prevalent in Bangladesh, with alarming statistics showing a significant number of cases in the Rangpur district. The HTN&RCR aims to address this issue by offering early detection, comprehensive care, and patient education, promoting a holistic approach to treatment.

Challenges and Humanitarian Journey:
The establishment of HTN&RCR is a humanitarian initiative aimed at providing relief to those suffering from Hypertension and Diabetes in Bangladesh. It continues to operate with a deep commitment to improving public health.

Prevalence of Hypertension and Diabetes:
Globally, Hypertension is a leading cause of premature death, with over 1.28 billion affected adults. In Bangladesh, the prevalence is concerning, with Rangpur district showing a higher-than-average rate of 33.3%. Hypertension is a leading contributor to stroke, heart disease, and kidney complications.

Core Values and Principles:
HTN&RCR operates with the core values of humanity, impartiality, neutrality, excellence, integrity, respect, empowerment, and inclusion. It emphasizes providing patient-centered care, conducting research, and offering services at minimal cost, particularly for vulnerable populations such as the poor and freedom fighters.

Slogan:
“Enjoy good health and well-being by keeping control of Hypertension and Diabetes Mellitus.”

Eligibility for Registration:
The center is open to individuals suffering from Hypertension or Diabetes, offering comprehensive medical services once they are registered.

address: Opposite National Traumatology Institute, Syed Mahbub, Sayed Mahbub Morshed Rd, Dhaka 1207

Sher-e-Bangla Medical College (SBMC), established in 1968, is a government medical institution located in Barisal, Bangladesh. It operates as a constituent college under the University of Dhaka.

SBMC offers the MBBS degree and provides comprehensive professional training and medical research opportunities through its 1,000-bed teaching hospital. Notably, MBBS degrees awarded from October 1984 onward are recognized by the General Medical Council (GMC) of the United Kingdom for overseas registration eligibility.

The college spans approximately 81.545 acres and houses extensive campus facilities, including student dormitories, a nursing institute, a nurses’ training center, staff nurse accommodations, and faculty residences.

With over 35 years of excellence, Labaid has revolutionized the healthcare landscape, providing high-quality diagnostics, specialized consultation, and advanced…
post_content:
Labaid is one of Bangladesh’s most prominent and trusted private healthcare brands, renowned for pioneering the country’s first super-specialty cardiac hospital. With over 35 years of excellence, Labaid has revolutionized the healthcare landscape, providing high-quality diagnostics, specialized consultation, and advanced treatments under one roof. Today, the brand serves over 3 million patients annually, employs nearly 8,000 professionals, and works with 1,000+ leading consultants nationwide.

Our Legacy

Founded in 1989 by visionary physician-entrepreneur Dr. A.M. Shamim, Labaid began with a mission to simplify access to healthcare by integrating diagnostics, consultations, and pharmacies into a single platform. What started as a diagnostic center in Dhaka has now grown into a diverse healthcare ecosystem.

In 2004, Labaid opened Labaid Cardiac Hospital, reducing the need for Bangladeshis to travel abroad for heart care. This was followed by Labaid Specialized Hospital (2006) and Labaid Cancer Hospital, delivering multidisciplinary, tertiary-level care with cutting-edge technology and a paperless medical record system.

Diversification & Innovation

Labaid’s commitment to improving lives extends beyond healthcare:

  • Pharmaceuticals: With its own manufacturing plant, Labaid ensures medicine quality and safety through direct supply chains and a cold-chain network of 25+ Labaid Pharmacies, setting a national benchmark in pharmaceutical retail.

  • Education: To address the country’s skill gap, Labaid founded:

    • State University of Bangladesh (2002)

    • Bangladesh Institute of Medical and Dental Technology (1996)

    • State College of Health Science (2006)
      These institutions train health professionals, nurses, dental technicians, and more.

  • Real Estate: Through Labaid Properties Ltd., the group builds reliable, modern homes and infrastructure to fulfill people’s dreams.

  • Agro & Food: Labaid Agros, established in 2004, delivers fresh, organic food products to both patients and general consumers.

  • Digital Healthcare: The upcoming myhealth.com platform aims to be a one-stop, digital healthcare hub—connecting hospitals, diagnostics centers, and pharmacies for streamlined appointment scheduling, prescription services, and more.

Our Vision

“Excellence through innovation” lies at the core of Labaid’s vision. We aim to continuously add value to our services, driven by strong customer relationships and adaptability in a fast-changing world.

Our Values

Labaid is guided by principles that reflect our deep commitment to humanity and our nation:

  • Integrity
    We uphold honesty, fairness, and transparency in all that we do.

  • Excellence
    We pursue the highest standards in service, technology, and innovation—believing excellence is a continuous journey.

  • Respect for Individuals
    We value people—patients, employees, partners, and communities—with dignity, openness, and mutual respect.
address: House # 19, Garib-E-Newaz Avenue, Sector 13, Uttara Model Town, Dhaka-1230

post_content:
Chattogram, the second-largest city in Bangladesh with a population of approximately 9.5 million, is located in the southeastern region of the country. Serving as the nation’s key industrial hub, Chattogram handles more than 65% of Bangladesh’s total import and export activities. The city is connected internationally via its own airport and through the capital, Dhaka.

Despite its economic significance, Chattogram still faces challenges in the diagnosis and treatment of life-threatening diseases. As a result, many residents are often compelled to travel to Dhaka or even abroad for advanced medical care. Belle Vue Hospital aims to address this gap by offering world-class healthcare services within the country. Equipped with advanced and well-established diagnostic laboratories, the hospital is dedicated to delivering reliable and comprehensive diagnostic solutions.

post_excerpt: East-West Medical College & Hospital is a prominent medical institution in Bangladesh, located at Aichi Nagar, JBCS Saroni, Khairtak, Turag, Dhaka. Nestled on the bank of the Turag River, the campus offers a serene natural environment just five kilometers from…
post_content:
East-West Medical College & Hospital is a prominent medical institution in Bangladesh, located at Aichi Nagar, JBCS Saroni, Khairtak, Turag, Dhaka. Nestled on the bank of the Turag River, the campus offers a serene natural environment just five kilometers from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport. The college aims to deliver high-quality, broad-based medical education with an emphasis on professional development. Its mission is to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and attitude required to address community health priorities and build a strong foundation for advanced training and future studies. The college operates under the Alim Foundation and is part of the Aichi Healthcare Group.

Aichi Healthcare Group was founded in 1996 by Prof. Dr. Moazzem Hossain, MBBS, STPS (England), Ph.D. (Japan). Dr. Hossain studied for four years at Nagoya University, Japan, with support from the Bangladesh government, and was awarded a Gold Medal by the Japanese government for his exceptional achievements. On returning to Bangladesh, he dedicated himself to advancing the nation’s healthcare to an international standard. Today, the group operates a medical college, dental university, nursing university, medical technology institute, and two hospitals, all committed to enhancing healthcare services across the country.
address: East West Medical College & Hospital, Nishat Nagar, Dhaka 1230

post_content:
The Journey of Natural Health

In 2002, a visionary young couple — Dr. Hasanuzzaman and Dr. Aziza Parveen — founded Natural Health, a medical service center in Satkhira, guided by a unique philosophy of compassionate care and modernized treatment. From the very beginning, they earned the trust and admiration of the local community through their dedication, medical excellence, and the use of advanced homeopathic tools, including the Radar Opus software.

Their center quickly became a beacon of hope, particularly for underprivileged patients, many of whom received treatment free of cost. Their integrity, empathy, and clinical success soon made them widely respected in the region.

Their reputation reached new heights when the local Deputy Commissioner and his wife sought treatment from them and, being thoroughly satisfied, recommended Natural Health to others in the district headquarters of Satkhira.

Driven by the growing demand and their passion to serve more people, the couple expanded their services by opening a new branch in Uttara, Dhaka in 2007. Through tireless effort and consistent quality, they managed both branches with excellence, gradually building Natural Health into a trusted name in homeopathic care across Bangladesh.
address: House 15, 1st west lane after Mascot Plaza, Road No 34,

post_excerpt: Jahurul Islam Medical College & Hospital was established with the mission to provide both essential and specialized medical care to people from all walks of life. The hospital began its journey in August 1989 with 250 beds and has since…
post_content:
Jahurul Islam Medical College & Hospital was established with the mission to provide both essential and specialized medical care to people from all walks of life. The hospital began its journey in August 1989 with 250 beds and has since expanded to over 500 beds, offering a wide range of medical and surgical services with state-of-the-art equipment and modern facilities. In its final phase of development, the hospital will house 750 beds, becoming a fully integrated medical center.

Strategically located in Bhagalpur-Bajitpur, the hospital laid the foundation for establishing a private medical college in the region. Its advanced infrastructure, equipped with modern instruments and resources, makes it an ideal environment for medical education, research, and practical training.

The institution is enriched by the expertise of distinguished professors, surgeons, and consultants from both home and abroad, ensuring high-quality education and patient care across the medical college, hospital, and nursing college.

To enhance operational efficiency, the hospital is fully integrated with a computer networking system. All departments are connected through LAN and WAN, enabling real-time data sharing and centralized information management via a central database server.

Key Facilities & Highlights:

  • More than 500-bed hospital (planned expansion to 750 beds)

  • Fully equipped Nursing College

  • Patient Attendant Dormitory

  • Doctors’ Dormitory & Quarters

  • Nurses’ Dormitory

  • Staff Residential Quarters

  • Central Mosque

  • Recreation and Community Centre

  • Central Cafeteria

  • Modern infrastructure and digital connectivity across departments

post_excerpt: The medical education system in Bangladesh has been steadily advancing to align with the global developments in medical science. Recognizing the need for a dynamic, motivated, and dedicated group of young professionals to lead the health sector in the 21st…
post_content:
The medical education system in Bangladesh has been steadily advancing to align with the global developments in medical science. Recognizing the need for a dynamic, motivated, and dedicated group of young professionals to lead the health sector in the 21st century, the Bangladesh Armed Forces established the Armed Forces Medical College (AFMC). This initiative aims to respond effectively to the evolving landscape of medical education and technology worldwide.

The Army Medical Corps (AMC) plays a crucial role in preserving the operational strength of the Armed Forces by delivering quality healthcare in both wartime and peacetime. To sustain this mission, the AMC requires a continuous supply of competent doctors. However, it has been observed that graduates from conventional medical colleges often do not meet the unique demands of military service. In response, the government founded AFMC to produce military-oriented, high-quality medical graduates. These professionals are not only prepared for service in the Armed Forces but also contribute significantly to the national healthcare system as disciplined and skilled physicians.

post_content:
Development for Education Society and Health (DESH) coalesced through the dynamic collaboration of impassioned social reformers, erudite educators, and distinguished medical practitioners, all united by an overarching commitment to providing comprehensive medical services. Their seminal objective was to propagate knowledge, social welfare, and healthcare provisions to marginalized sectors of society. In pursuit of this noble mission, Chattagram International Medical College and Hospital (CIMCH) was inaugurated in 2013 under the aegis of these luminary figures, aiming to democratize access to the esteemed field of medical education.

Since its inception, CIMCH has ascended as a bastion of confidence in healthcare provision within its locale. It has garnered acclaim for its steadfast dedication to addressing the healthcare exigencies of the regional population. CIMCH has played a pivotal role in advancing the governmental agenda for health development, foregrounding education and research as pivotal instruments for progress. Through its multifaceted endeavors, CIMCH has rendered indubitable contributions in augmenting healthcare services and realizing the overarching objectives for health development articulated by government.

post_excerpt: মাতৃ ও শিশু স্বাস্থ্য প্রশিক্ষণ প্রতিষ্ঠান এবং ২০০ শয্যা বিশিষ্ট মাতৃ ও শিশু স্বাস্থ্য হাসপাতাল — স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার কল্যাণ মন্ত্রণালয়ের স্বাস্থ্য শিক্ষা ও পরিবার কল্যাণ বিভাগের আওতাধীন পরিবার পরিকল্পনা অধিদপ্তরের অধীন একটি সরকারি প্রতিষ্ঠান। প্রথম পর্যায়ে, সম্পূর্ণ সরকারি অর্থায়নে…
post_content:
মাতৃ ও শিশু স্বাস্থ্য প্রশিক্ষণ প্রতিষ্ঠান এবং ২০০ শয্যা বিশিষ্ট মাতৃ ও শিশু স্বাস্থ্য হাসপাতাল — স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার কল্যাণ মন্ত্রণালয়ের স্বাস্থ্য শিক্ষা ও পরিবার কল্যাণ বিভাগের আওতাধীন পরিবার পরিকল্পনা অধিদপ্তরের অধীন একটি সরকারি প্রতিষ্ঠান।

প্রথম পর্যায়ে, সম্পূর্ণ সরকারি অর্থায়নে ২০০৬ সালে “Establishment of Maternal and Child Health Training and Research Institute (MCHTRI) at Lalkuthi, Mirpur, Dhaka” শীর্ষক প্রকল্পের মাধ্যমে প্রতিষ্ঠানটির নির্মাণ কাজ শুরু হয়। তবে প্রকল্পটি অসমাপ্ত অবস্থায় ২০০৯ সালের জুন মাসে শেষ হয়।

পরবর্তী পর্যায়ে, “লালকুঠি মাতৃ ও শিশু স্বাস্থ্য কেন্দ্রের অসম্পূর্ণ কাজ সমাপ্তকরণ” শীর্ষক একটি প্রকল্পের মাধ্যমে পুনরায় নির্মাণ কাজ শুরু হয়, যা জানুয়ারি ২০১৬ থেকে জুন ২০১৯ সাল পর্যন্ত চলমান ছিল। প্রকল্পটি ৩০ জুন ২০১৯ তারিখে সফলভাবে সম্পন্ন হয়।

প্রতিষ্ঠানটিতে রয়েছে:

  • ১০ তলা বিশিষ্ট একটি হাসপাতাল ভবন
  • ৬ তলা বিশিষ্ট ৩টি আবাসিক ভবন

প্রকল্প সমাপ্তির পর ১ জুলাই ২০১৯ তারিখ থেকে প্রতিষ্ঠানটি রাজস্বখাতে অন্তর্ভুক্ত হয় এবং বহিঃবিভাগের মাধ্যমে সীমিত পরিসরে মাতৃ, শিশু, প্রজনন স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার পরিকল্পনা সেবা কার্যক্রম শুরু হয়।

পরবর্তীতে, কোভিড-১৯ মহামারির সময়ে স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার কল্যাণ মন্ত্রণালয়ের নির্দেশক্রমে ১০ এপ্রিল ২০২০ থেকে ১৫ সেপ্টেম্বর ২০২০ পর্যন্ত এই প্রতিষ্ঠানকে নিবেদিত কোভিড হাসপাতাল হিসেবে ব্যবহৃত করা হয়।

১ অক্টোবর ২০২০ তারিখ থেকে পুনরায় বহিঃবিভাগের মাধ্যমে নিয়মিত স্বাস্থ্যসেবা কার্যক্রম চালু হয়। বর্তমানে প্রতিষ্ঠানটিতে বহিঃবিভাগ ও আন্তঃবিভাগ উভয় সেবাই চলমান রয়েছে।

post_content:
২৫০ শয্যা বিশিষ্ট টিবি হাসপাতাল, ঢাকার শেরে বাংলা নগর, শ্যামলীতে অবস্থিত, যা যক্ষা ও অন্যান্য বক্ষব্যাধি রোগের চিকিৎসায় একটি তৃতীয় স্তরের কেন্দ্র। এখানে যক্ষা রোগের জন্য সর্বোচ্চ মানের চিকিৎসা সেবা প্রদান করা হয়। এই হাসপাতালটি ল্যাব টেকনিশিয়ান এবং সরকারি-বেসরকারি মেডিকেল শিক্ষার্থীদের প্রশিক্ষণ এবং গবেষণায়ও সহায়তা প্রদান করে।

১৯৬৩ সালে যক্ষা নিয়ন্ত্রণ প্রকল্প হিসেবে শুরু হওয়া এই হাসপাতালটি ১৯৭১ সালের স্বাধীনতার পর জাতীয় টিবি কন্ট্রোল প্রজেক্ট, শ্যামলী নামে পরিচিত হয়। তখন থেকে এটি যক্ষা রোগীদের পরীক্ষা এবং বহির্বিভাগ সেবা প্রদান করে আসছিল। ২০১৫ সালে একটি নতুন ৪ তলা ভবন নির্মাণের পর এর নামকরণ করা হয় ২৫০ শয্যা বিশিষ্ট টিবি হাসপাতাল। ২০১৭ সালের জানুয়ারি মাসে সম্মানিত স্বাস্থ্য ও পরিবার পরিকল্পনা মন্ত্রী মোহাম্মদ নাসিম এখানে অন্তর্বিভাগ সেবার উদ্বোধন করেন। বর্তমানে এখানে ১৫০ বেডের ইনডোর ভর্তি সুবিধা, ফ্রি বেড, পেয়িং বেড, কেবিন, ২৪ ঘণ্টা ইমার্জেন্সি সেবা, অপারেশন থিয়েটার, থোরাসিক সার্জারি, জিন এক্সপার্ট টেস্ট, এলপিএ, লিকুইড কালচার, স্পাইরোমেট্রি, ফাইবার অপটিক ব্রঙ্কোস্কোপি এবং ফ্লুরোস্কোপি ফর প্লুরাল বায়োপসির সুযোগ রয়েছে।

এই হাসপাতালটি নিগেটিভ প্রেশার আইসিইউ, এইচডিইউ, এমডিআর, পুরুষ ও মহিলা ওয়ার্ড এবং নিগেটিভ প্রেশার বায়োসেফটি লেভেল ৩ ল্যাবরেটরি সেবাও প্রদান করে। সম্প্রতি এখানে নিগেটিভ প্রেশার এমডিআর পুরুষ ও মহিলা ওয়ার্ড চালু করা হয়েছে। বহির্বিভাগে সেবা নিতে রোগীদের মাত্র ১০ টাকার টিকিটে এই সেবা পাওয়া যায়, যা সকাল ৮:৩০ থেকে দুপুর ২:৩০ পর্যন্ত, শুক্রবার ছাড়া সপ্তাহে ৬ দিন উপলব্ধ।

অ্যাজমা সেন্টারে রয়েছে হেলথ এডুকেশন রুম, যা রোগীদের চিকিৎসা সংক্রান্ত দিকনির্দেশনা প্রদান করে এবং এখানে অ্যাজমা সম্পর্কিত হ্যান্ডবিল এবং প্রকাশনা পাওয়া যায়। কোভিড-১৯ মহামারীর সময় এই হাসপাতালটি কোভিড রোগীদের আইসিইউ সেবা, নিগেটিভ প্রেশার আইসিইউ, আরটি-পিসিআর টেস্ট এবং সর্বোচ্চ চিকিৎসা সেবা প্রদান করেছিল।

ফ্রি বেড, পেয়িং বেড, কেবিন এবং ৪০% ফ্রি বেড সুবিধা, বিশেষত গরিব মানুষের জন্য, ছাড়াও এখানে স্বল্প ব্যয়ে পরীক্ষা-নিরীক্ষার ব্যবস্থা রয়েছে।

The aim of Tairunnessa Memorial Medical College is to provide a comprehensive education that emphasizes professional growth, equipping students with the knowledge, skills, and attitudes needed to address community health priorities. The college focuses on preparing students for future training and studies, ensuring they develop a strong foundation for their medical careers. Students will:

  • Develop proficiency in essential clinical skills, including the ability to take patient histories, conduct thorough clinical examinations, interpret findings, and perform basic medical procedures with competence.
  • Cultivate the attitudes necessary to achieve high standards in clinical practice, both in individual and population care, as well as fostering personal development.

post_excerpt: Southern Medical College & Hospital (SMCH) was established in 2005 with the mission to produce a generation of highly skilled medical graduates dedicated to providing compassionate and cost-effective care to those in need. Our goal is to offer the highest…
post_content:
Southern Medical College & Hospital (SMCH) was established in 2005 with the mission to produce a generation of highly skilled medical graduates dedicated to providing compassionate and cost-effective care to those in need. Our goal is to offer the highest quality treatment to every patient, ensuring their satisfaction. The institution’s primary objective is to uphold high standards in medical education, train medical personnel, and conduct research across various medical disciplines. Our vision extends beyond producing doctors—we aim to shape compassionate, responsible human beings.

The need for more medical colleges in Bangladesh is pressing, and to provide a dynamic learning environment, we have partnered with international medical institutions for real-time lessons through teleconferencing and video conferencing. Our faculty members are not just educators, but mentors guiding our students every step of the way. In addition to training medical professionals, our institution offers training for paramedical staff, as we are committed to improving the quality of healthcare and preparing individuals to meet the health needs of others.

Our decision to focus on healthcare stems from the shortage of physicians in Bangladesh and the inability of many people to afford quality treatment. With many suffering from various illnesses, we could not remain indifferent to their plight. This led to the establishment of our medical college, equipped with modern facilities and a charitable approach to treatment, ensuring that even the underprivileged have access to the best possible care without discrimination.

Our hospital is functioning well, and we are in the process of developing specialized departments, starting with Nephrology. We have already initiated the setup of a kidney dialysis facility, which is currently available in only two hospitals in Chittagong. However, many low-income individuals lack access to such services. Southern Medical College & Hospital is always open to providing this care to those who are otherwise deprived. Additionally, we are planning to enhance our Orthopedic Department, as Chattogram still lags behind in this area. Although our journey has not been free of challenges, we have overcome many obstacles and remain steadfast in our mission.

We continue to strive toward progress, but we need the support of people from all walks of life, especially parents and students, to help make this noble endeavor a success. We believe that, through mutual cooperation, we can create a better future for all.

post_excerpt: Here’s a refined and polished version of your text while maintaining its essence and structure: A Message of Gratitude and Progress We are forever grateful to Allah (S.W.T.) for His guidance and blessings, which have enabled us to reach where…
post_content:
Here’s a refined and polished version of your text while maintaining its essence and structure:

A Message of Gratitude and Progress

We are forever grateful to Allah (S.W.T.) for His guidance and blessings, which have enabled us to reach where we are today.

Our message begins with a heartfelt **”Thank You”**—to the communities we serve, our partners across the healthcare system, and the dedicated staff, physicians, and volunteers who demonstrate unwavering commitment to our patients and their families every day. Your efforts, especially during the most challenging times, have been invaluable.

This year, PDCL celebrates its 35th Anniversary. Over these three and a half decades, we have grown immensely as an organization. What began in a modest three-room rented space with basic equipment has evolved into an advanced diagnostic center serving hundreds of thousands of patients annually.

The passion and dedication of the PDCL team drive innovation and ensure the delivery of quality care with exceptional outcomes. Our mission and vision remain steadfast: to serve the ailing members of society with compassion and excellence. After 35 years of service, we recognize that quality is an ongoing journey, and there is still much to achieve as we strive for continuous improvement.

May Allah (S.W.T.) bless us all as we continue this noble mission.

Milestones in Our Journey

1986

  • Began operations in a small rented house equipped with a Colorimeter, Hot Water Oven, X-Ray, ECG, and Ultrasonogram machines.

1987

  • Integrated an Olympus GI Endoscopy machine and computer systems.

1988

  • Introduced the first Elisa Reader in Bangladesh, marking a significant technological advancement.

1991

  • Construction began on our own building.

1992

  • Installed a TV monitor-enabled X-Ray machine and established our own water distillation plant.

1993

  • Implemented a Hospital Information System (HIS) for billing, report preparation, and combined reporting. Established a server to store patient information.

1994

  • Installed a 316 K.V. power backup generator, one of the first among private diagnostic centers in Bangladesh.

1995

  • Added the Fully Automated Biochemistry Analyzer Hitachi 911 from German ROCHE GmbH, the first of its kind in Bangladesh.

1996

  • Introduced the Semi-Automated Immunochemistry ES300.

2000

  • Launched Chemiluminescent Technology (IMMULITE-One) and the country’s only privately owned Automated Blood Cell Separator system.

2001

  • Added an ETT machine, Sysmex Automated Blood Cell Counter, and Blood Coagulation Analyzer.

2003

  • Established CR technology and a self-contained PCR lab. Installed COBAS AMPLICOR AUTOMATED PCR, another first in Bangladesh.

2006

  • Attached the Olympus AU640 Biochemistry Analyzer.

2007

  • Added the DPC IMMULITE 2000, an Automated Immuno Chemistry Analyzer.

2008

  • Installed a Digital X-Ray Machine (1000 mA Shimadzu Corporation), Hematology Analyzer XT 2000i, and a drinking water plant.

2009

  • Introduced Automated Histopathology, Digital OPG, and the Olympus AU680 Automated Biochemistry Analyzer.

2010

  • Adopted several cutting-edge technologies, including GE Medical System’s High-Definition Open MRI, Multi-Slice CT Scan, VIVID-7 Pro, IMMULITE 2000XPi, Architect i1000SR, ETT machine, and PACS server.

2012

  • Installed the Sebia Capillary 2 Flex Piercing Electrophoresis machine.

2013

  • Launched the Hematology Analyzer XN-3000, the first unit of its kind in Bangladesh.

2014

  • Introduced the Apelem Platinum 1000 mA low-radiation X-Ray machine (the first in South Asia), GE Voluson E8 Ultrasound, Mindray DC8 Ultrasound, Spirometry Thor Tube, and MMS Flow Master Uroflowmetry.

2015

  • Added the Apelem Stratos dR BMD machine, EEG machine Nihon Kohden 1200 J/K, Automated ESR Analyzer (Mechatronics; STARRSED), and Canon Alexion Multi-Slice CT Scan.

2017

  • Installed the DL Automated Blood Culture System, Mindray DC8 Expert Echo-Cardiogram, Resona 7 Ultrasound, and Toshiba 1.5 Tesla Vantage Elan MRI machine.

2019

  • Introduced the Diasorin Liaison XL Fully Automated machine.

2021

  • Launched the Vivid E95 with a 4D probe (Echo machine), Capillary 3 Octa (advanced over Sebia Capillary 2 Flex Piercing), 12-Channel 24-Hour Holter Monitoring (Nihon Kohden, Japan), and FibroScan Compact 530 with CAP (Echosens) featuring an XL probe.

Looking Ahead

As we reflect on our journey, we remain committed to innovation, quality, and compassionate care. We thank Allah (S.W.T.) for His blessings and guidance and look forward to continuing our mission of serving humanity with excellence.

Let me know if you’d like further refinements!

We are a leading private healthcare provider in Bangladesh, recognized for our commitment to excellence in medical treatment and specialized healthcare services. As a pioneer in establishing the country’s first super-specialty cardiac hospital, we have played a key role in transforming the private healthcare landscape. Our extensive network of medical centers across Bangladesh ensures high-quality diagnostics and expert consultations, serving millions of patients annually with the support of thousands of dedicated professionals and top-tier physicians.

Under the leadership of a visionary healthcare entrepreneur, we have spent over three decades redefining corporate healthcare in Bangladesh. By creating a robust platform for local medical experts, establishing centers of excellence, and delivering advanced treatments—including complex procedures such as transplantation and cardiovascular surgeries—our efforts have helped instill confidence that world-class healthcare is accessible within the country.

Beyond healthcare, we have expanded into various sectors to add value to people’s lives. Our pharmaceutical division ensures access to high-quality medicines, while our educational institutions address the need for skilled professionals in healthcare and beyond. Additionally, our real estate ventures contribute to modern infrastructure development, and our agricultural initiatives promote organic, high-quality food for both patients and consumers.

With a strong reputation for reliability and innovation, we remain dedicated to serving the people of Bangladesh, continuously striving to uphold our commitment to excellence, trust, and progress.

post_excerpt: Chevron Clinical Laboratory Ltd. (CCLL), Bangladesh’s first homegrown healthcare group, was established on February 22, 1984, and began operations as the country's first integrated investigative laboratory on May 16, 1984, in Chittagong. It was a pioneering step in bringing automated…
post_content:
Chevron Clinical Laboratory Ltd. (CCLL), Bangladesh’s first homegrown healthcare group, was established on February 22, 1984, and began operations as the country’s first integrated investigative laboratory on May 16, 1984, in Chittagong. It was a pioneering step in bringing automated and integrated diagnostics to the nation.

Since its inception, Chevron has provided comprehensive diagnostic services, helping millions of patients receive accurate and reliable medical insights. With an extensive catalog of clinical laboratory procedures, its services support disease diagnosis, treatment planning, and healthcare management.

Each year, Chevron Clinical Lab serves one in three Chittagong residents and collaborates with over three-fourths of the region’s physicians. A team of 500 dedicated professionals ensures that high-quality diagnostics contribute to better patient care and improved health outcomes.

Today, Chevron’s portfolio extends beyond laboratory services, covering Clinical Laboratory Management, Healthcare Technical Services, Diagnostic Modality Development, Specialist Physician Consultations, a modern Eye Hospital and Research Center, and more, reinforcing its commitment to advancing healthcare in Bangladesh.

post_content:
CSCR (Pvt) Ltd. has been a trusted name in clinical laboratory services since 1999, setting a benchmark in patient care with its commitment to precision and accuracy in diagnostics. Operating 24/7, it stands as a premier facility equipped with cutting-edge technology and state-of-the-art machinery, ensuring world-class diagnostic services.

Beyond laboratory testing, CSCR also provides consultation services from some of the country’s most renowned physicians and surgeons. Over the years, it has earned the trust of both patients and healthcare professionals by delivering high-quality, prompt, and reliable diagnostic services. From routine clinical tests to emergency cardiac enzyme analyses and advanced molecular diagnostics, CSCR remains at the forefront of medical diagnostics in Bangladesh.

What sets CSCR apart is its round-the-clock availability, making it the only diagnostic center in Bangladesh to operate 24/7 for public service. Quality assurance and a robust laboratory information system are integral to its operations, ensuring efficiency and accuracy.

A team of highly trained experts, including Pathologists, Hematologists, Histopathologists, Microbiologists, Immunologists, Biochemists, Molecular Biologists, and Laboratory Managers, work under strict supervision to maintain reliability and deliver prompt test results, reinforcing CSCR’s reputation as a leader in diagnostic healthcare.

post_content: The Academic Council is the highest academic body of the college, with the Principal serving as its Chairman. The Council members include the Vice-Principal, Phase Coordinators, Heads of Departments, faculty members up to Assistant Professors, and two selected student representatives—one male and one female—from each phase. This body plays a crucial role in overseeing and ensuring the smooth progress of both academic and administrative activities on campus. The Principal, as the primary patron of the Council, typically calls meetings once a month, though he may call additional meetings if an emergency arises.

post_content:
ইহা একটি বিশেষজ্ঞ ডেন্টাল ডাক্তারের চেম্বার। প্রধান ডাক্তার হচ্ছেন: ডাঃ মোঃ আব্দুর রশীদ মন্ডল, বিডিএস, বিসিএস (স্বাস্থ্য), এমএস, এফসিপিএস (ডিজারটেশন)। তিনি বর্তমানে ঢাকা ডেন্টাল কলেজের সহকারী অধ্যাপক। ডেন্টাল সার্জন ও অর্থোডনটিস্ট হিসেবে তিনি সকল প্রকার দন্তরোগ, আঁকাবাঁকা দাত, উঁচু-নিচু দাত বা ফাঁকা দাতের সমস্যা সমাধানে বিশেষজ্ঞ।

শিক্ষাগত যোগ্যতাঃ
১. বিডিএস (ঢাকা ডেন্টাল কলেজ, ১৯৯৭-২০০৩, ৫ বছর মেয়াদি)
২. পিজিটি (ঢাকা ডেন্টাল কলেজ, ২০০৪, ১ বছর মেয়াদি পোস্ট গ্রাজুয়েশন ট্রেনিং)
৩. বিসিএস (স্বাস্থ্য), ১৫ তম বিসিএস এর মাধ্যমে ২০০৬ সালে নিয়োগপত্র
৪. এফসিপিএস পার্ট-১, অর্থোডন্টিক্স বিষয়ে, জানুয়ারি ২০০৭
৫. এমএস (অর্থোডনটিক্স) – বঙ্গবন্ধু শেখ মুজিব মেডিকেল বিশ্ববিদ্যালয় থেকে ২০১৫ সালে ৫ বছর মেয়াদি এমএস ডিগ্রি অর্জন

ডাঃ মোঃ আব্দুর রশীদ মন্ডল বর্তমানে ঢাকা ডেন্টাল কলেজে সহকারী অধ্যাপক হিসেবে কর্মরত আছেন এবং বিশেষজ্ঞ ডেন্টাল চিকিৎসা সেবা প্রদান করছেন।

post_content:
Dhaka Medical College is one of the oldest and most prestigious medical institutions in Bangladesh, located in the heart of the capital, Dhaka. It is situated in the Bakshibazar area, close to both the University of Dhaka and the Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET).

The idea of establishing a medical college in Dhaka was first proposed to the British government in 1939, but the plan was delayed due to World War II. However, after the war, a committee was formed in 1945, and based on its recommendations, Dhaka Medical College officially opened its doors on July 10, 1946. This marked the beginning of a new era in medical education in Bangladesh, with Dhaka Medical College playing a key role in training doctors and healthcare professionals for the country.

post_excerpt: Dhaka National Medical College has a rich history, dating back to 1925, when it was originally founded as the Dhaka National Medical Institute. It was part of the broader Non-cooperation movement against British colonial rule in the Indian subcontinent, which…
post_content:
Dhaka National Medical College has a rich history, dating back to 1925, when it was originally founded as the Dhaka National Medical Institute. It was part of the broader Non-cooperation movement against British colonial rule in the Indian subcontinent, which was led by prominent leaders like Mahatma Gandhi, Mawlana Muhammad Ali, and Mawlana Shaukat Ali. This movement encouraged people to establish their own educational institutions and boycott those run by the British government.

In response to this, two medical schools were founded in the Bengal province—one in Calcutta, known as the Calcutta National Medical Institute, and the other in Dhaka, which became the Dhaka National Medical Institute.

The Dhaka National Medical Institute was established near Bahadur Shah Park in Dhaka on land donated by Zaminder Raghunath Das. Many nationalists contributed financially to its establishment. The first governing body was chaired by the renowned scientist Sir Prafulla Chandra Roy KBE, and included notable nationalist leaders like Sir Khwaja Nazim Uddin, former Chief Minister of Bengal, and Dr. J.C. Ghosh, the founder of the Ayuraveda Institute, among others.

The institute initially offered a four-year medical course (LMF) in modern medicine and also established a general hospital to provide clinical training for students and healthcare services to the local population.

In 1958, the four-year medical course (LMF) was replaced by a five-year MBBS course by the East Pakistan provincial government. However, due to lack of support from the Pakistan government, Dhaka National Medical Institute was unable to offer the MBBS course and became a general hospital by 1965.

During the Liberation War of Bangladesh in 1971, the Dhaka National Medical Institute Hospital provided refuge to the freedom fighters. After the war, the hospital faced financial difficulties and was on the brink of collapse. It was then that Father of the Nation Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman stepped in, allocating Taka thirty-five lacs to restore the hospital and forming an Adhoc Governing Body under the leadership of Mr. M. Sirajul Islam, MLA.

The government also introduced a rule where a Management Board would be formed every four years, and the hospital would receive a yearly financial grant from the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare. The initial grant was Taka two crore, which has since increased to Taka five crore. In return, the hospital treats 40% of its patients for free.

In 1994, the idea of establishing a medical college with the Dhaka National Medical Institute Hospital came to fruition. The Dhaka National Medical College was formally inaugurated on October 15, 1995, and its academic building was inaugurated on November 25, 2006. The college operates in accordance with the rules and regulations of the University of Dhaka, the Bangladesh Medical and Dental Council (BM&DC), and the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.

Dhaka National Medical College is a non-political, non-profit institution and now has a modern 11-story academic building with adequate parking facilities. Graduates of the college are eligible for limited registration with the General Medical Council of the United Kingdom and can also take the United States Medical License Examinations (USMLE).

The overall management of the college is overseen by a governing body formed by the University of Dhaka. Currently, the chairman of the governing body is Advocate Kazi Firoz Rashid, MP, who is leading efforts to build a digital Bangladesh as part of the vision outlined by Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.

post_excerpt: Dhaka Shishu (Children) Hospital is the largest children's hospital in Bangladesh and is a government-supported tertiary care facility with 640 beds dedicated to the healthcare of children. The hospital was established just a few months after Bangladesh's liberation, in March…
post_content:
Dhaka Shishu (Children) Hospital is the largest children’s hospital in Bangladesh and is a government-supported tertiary care facility with 640 beds dedicated to the healthcare of children.

The hospital was established just a few months after Bangladesh’s liberation, in March 1972, as the country’s only specialized children’s hospital. Initially, it began its services in a private house in Dhanmondi, Dhaka, with only 50 indoor beds. The Out-Patient Department (OPD) was set up in a tent in nearby Sukrabad, Dhaka, at the same time. The hospital received crucial early financial support from organizations like “Save the Children Fund, UK” and later “World Vision, Bangladesh.”

In December 1974, the National Economic Council (NEC) of Bangladesh, chaired by the Father of the Nation, Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, approved a plan to build a 250-bed children’s hospital, with an expansion capacity to 650 beds. Following this, in March 1975, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare transferred the project site to its current location in Sher-e-Bangla Nagar, Shyamoli, Dhaka.

Dhaka Shishu Hospital began operations at this new site in June 1977. The construction was funded through government grants and the DSH trust fund, which was raised through donations, public lotteries, and concerts. The project was supervised by a committee from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, with notable figures such as the late Prof. Tofayel Ahmed, the Founder Member Secretary of the Management Board, being involved in the construction committee.

On January 17, 2012, Honorable Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina laid the foundation stone for a new 9-story building for the hospital. At the same time, she inaugurated the Pediatric Cardiac Center, which includes cardiac surgery, a cath-lab, and a cardiac ICU. This facility is the first of its kind for children in the country, offering specialized heart care for young patients.

Dhaka Shishu Hospital continues to be a cornerstone of pediatric healthcare in Bangladesh, providing vital services to children across the nation.

The hospital offers a total of 50 beds, with 40 dedicated to in-patient care and 10 for day care patients. The in-patient specialties include general medicine, general surgery, and gynaecology. Additionally, Dogma Hospital features an Urgent Care Centre, which includes both a Local Injuries Unit and a Medical Assessment Unit for immediate care.

Here’s a quick overview of the services we offer:

  • Consultant Out-Patient Clinics
  • Diagnostic Radiology and Laboratory services

  • Clinical Nurse Specialists in areas like diabetes, continence management, respiratory care, health promotion, infection control, palliative care, and pain management

At Dogma Hospital, we aim to provide comprehensive healthcare with a focus on patient well-being and comfort.

post_title: M H Samorita Hospital & Medical College
post_excerpt: Bangladesh is one of the most densely populated countries in the world, with a population of around 160 million. The country has a unique healthcare system, starting from the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare (MOH&FW) at the capital level,…
post_content:
Bangladesh is one of the most densely populated countries in the world, with a population of around 160 million. The country has a unique healthcare system, starting from the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare (MOH&FW) at the capital level, down to health assistants at the grassroots. However, due to budget constraints, healthcare coverage is not adequate or uniform across the country. This highlights the need for privatization in healthcare services, which is critical for Bangladesh’s future.

With this vision in mind, MH Samorita Hospital & Medical College, Dhaka, began its journey in 2010, with approval from the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare and the University of Dhaka. All praise is due to Allah for this accomplishment.

MH Samorita Medical College and its Dental Unit have taken significant steps to address the gap in healthcare education and provide opportunities for students in the current context.

The medical college is supported by a 500-bed teaching hospital, which is well-equipped and offers both general and specialized services, including an ICU, Cath Lab, CT Scan, CCU, NICU, HDU, and Dialysis unit.

The challenge ahead is to establish the Medical College and attached hospital as a center of excellence, ensuring quality, affordability, and satisfaction for all stakeholders while respecting our cultural values. Over the past seven years, the institution has successfully navigated these challenges and is now confident in its ability to educate both national and international students.

Students who join the college will undergo a comprehensive five-year program, eventually graduating as physicians ready to serve humanity with deep care and competence.

post_title: National Institute of Cancer Research & Hospital
post_excerpt: The National Institute of Cancer Research & Hospital (NICRH) provides a vibrant and supportive environment, staffed by highly trained professionals dedicated to managing cancer patients, education, and research. It is the only tertiary-level center in the country offering multidisciplinary cancer…
post_content:
The National Institute of Cancer Research & Hospital (NICRH) provides a vibrant and supportive environment, staffed by highly trained professionals dedicated to managing cancer patients, education, and research. It is the only tertiary-level center in the country offering multidisciplinary cancer care.

NICRH began its journey in 1982 in a tin-shade building at Dhaka Medical College Hospital. In 1986, it was moved to its current location in Mohakhali. Through a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Health and the Rotary Club of Dhaka, the Rotary Cancer Detection Unit (RCDU) was established, and the institute initially functioned as an outdoor cancer detection unit. In 1991, the institute added 50 indoor beds, and in 1994, it was officially renamed the National Institute of Cancer Research & Hospital (NICRH). The first radiation treatment was delivered in 1995 using a Cobalt 60 Teletherapy machine.

With support from the Saudi Fund for Development (SFD), the institute was upgraded to a 300-bed hospital, completing this expansion in 2015. Today, NICRH is a leading center for cancer treatment and research in Bangladesh.
address: Mohakhali, TB Gate Road, Tejgaon, Dhaka, 1212

post_content:
The National Institute of Cardiovascular Diseases (NICVD) is a leading institute in Bangladesh for the treatment of cardiovascular diseases. Established in 1978, its primary goal was to introduce modern cardiovascular care services in the country and to train skilled professionals to meet the growing healthcare needs.

In addition to providing the best possible care for heart patients, the NICVD also started offering Post Graduate medical courses in July 1982. Over the years, 166 doctors have completed their MD in Cardiology, 99 doctors have earned their MS in Cardiovascular & Thoracic Surgery, and 178 doctors have obtained their D. Card (Diploma in Cardiology) from the institute. Many of these professionals are now working in specialized cardiac care roles in both government and private hospitals across Bangladesh and internationally.

NICVD also conducts training programs for nurses, including those working in CCU, ICU, Cath Lab, and OT, as well as for other support staff. The institute also offers training for non-government doctors to enhance the overall healthcare service in the community.

post_title: National Institute of Diseases of the Chest & Hospital
post_excerpt: The National Institute of Diseases of the Chest and Hospital (NIDCH) is located in Mohakhali, Dhaka. It was originally established in 1955 as the "TB Hospital." After the Liberation War in 1971, it was upgraded to an institute. NIDCH focuses…
post_content:
The National Institute of Diseases of the Chest and Hospital (NIDCH) is located in Mohakhali, Dhaka. It was originally established in 1955 as the “TB Hospital.” After the Liberation War in 1971, it was upgraded to an institute. NIDCH focuses on four main objectives:

  1. To provide the highest standard of treatment for chest diseases and tuberculosis.
  2. To offer training and support for future Post Graduate Doctors.
  3. To conduct research related to chest diseases and tuberculosis.
  4. To raise awareness among the general population and spread current knowledge about tuberculosis and chest diseases to general physicians.

The hospital has 670 beds, with half of them dedicated to the Department of Respiratory Medicine. The department is divided into 12 units, while the Thoracic Surgery Department has 5 units. Each unit is led by a Professor or Associate Professor. NIDCH also has a fully equipped Asthma Center.

Bangladesh ENT Specialized Hospital & Training Center is a dedicated facility for the treatment of ear, nose, and throat (Otorhinolaryngology) diseases, located in Tejgaon, Dhaka. The construction of this institute began in 2009, and the newly built 12-story structure was officially inaugurated by Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina on June 19, 2013.

Once fully operational, the hospital will feature 250 beds and state-of-the-art facilities, including an international standard audio-vestibular lab, a sleep lab, and a voice study lab. This specialized institute will not only provide advanced medical care but also serve as a key training center, educating healthcare professionals in the diagnosis and treatment of ENT disorders, and thus improving the overall standard of care in the country.

The National Institute of Kidney Diseases & Urology (NIKDU) was established to address the growing need for better treatment facilities for patients suffering from kidney and urological diseases, as well as those requiring dialysis and transplantation. The need for such an institute became clear due to the insufficient treatment options available at the time.

The project is fully financed by the Government of the People’s Republic of Bangladesh, reflecting the government’s commitment to improving healthcare services for kidney and urological patients across the country. NIKDU aims to provide specialized care, advanced treatments, and much-needed support to patients facing these critical health challenges.

post_title: National Institute of Mental Health & Hospital
post_excerpt: The National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) is the leading state agency in Bangladesh dedicated to the treatment of mental health issues, as well as the development of mental health policies, research, and the training of mental health professionals. Located…
post_content:
The National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) is the leading state agency in Bangladesh dedicated to the treatment of mental health issues, as well as the development of mental health policies, research, and the training of mental health professionals.

Located in Sher-e-Bangla Nagar, Dhaka, NIMH was established in 2001 to meet the growing healthcare needs of Bangladesh’s diverse population, which exceeds 164 million people. Before this, from 1981, the institute functioned as the Institute of Mental Health & Research at various medical college hospitals in Dhaka.

This state-of-the-art facility employs over 300 staff members and provides comprehensive services, including outpatient, inpatient, and emergency care for both child and adult mental health disorders. As the largest teaching psychiatric hospital in Bangladesh, NIMH plays a vital role in training future leaders in psychiatry, psychology, and medicine. It also maintains a strong focus on research and the development of mental health policies to improve the country’s mental healthcare system.

NIMH is a key part of the Directorate General of Health Services, which is under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare of the Bangladesh Government. Through its efforts, NIMH is shaping the future of mental health care in the country and working toward a healthier, more informed society.

post_excerpt: The National Institute of Neurosciences & Hospital, with a 450-bed capacity, started its journey in September 2012 and was officially inaugurated by Honourable Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. Located in the health hub of Sher-e-Bangla Nagar, Dhaka, it is the only…
post_content:
The National Institute of Neurosciences & Hospital, with a 450-bed capacity, started its journey in September 2012 and was officially inaugurated by Honourable Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. Located in the health hub of Sher-e-Bangla Nagar, Dhaka, it is the only government-run tertiary care neuroscience center in Bangladesh.

This 10-story hospital offers specialized departments in Neurology, Neurosurgery, Pediatric Neurology, Pediatric Neurosurgery, Neurophysiology, Neurointervention, Neurorehabilitation, Neuroradiology, Neuropathology, Transfusion Medicine, Critical Care Medicine, and more. We have state-of-the-art facilities, including modern Operation Theatres, Cath Labs, Laboratory Services, ICU & HDU, and advanced technologies for Neurophysiology and Neuroradiology.

Our hospital provides specialized outpatient services and 24×7 emergency care. In addition, we offer inpatient services, all at minimal cost or free of charge, as per government regulations. Every day, over a thousand patients benefit from our specialized outpatient services. We run clinics for Stroke, Headache, Epilepsy, Movement Disorders, and Botox Therapy. Our 100-bed Comprehensive Stroke Unit is also operational.

Academically, we are proud of our highly qualified faculty members, and we offer residency training and postgraduate courses for MD, MS, and FCPS students, in collaboration with BSMMU and BCPS. We also conduct regular training programs for physicians working at the primary and secondary levels to help them understand and manage various neurological disorders, in line with the National Health Service policy.

At the National Institute of Neurosciences, we encourage research activities in the field of health sciences and organize scientific seminars, symposiums, and workshops, bringing together national and international experts. We have already conducted the National Epilepsy & Stroke Survey, and we are currently working on a clinical trial for GBS in collaboration with ICDDR-B and other international research organizations.

Our dedication to excellence has been recognized, and we are proud to have received the Best Performance Health Minister Award in 2017, 2018, 2019, and 2020 for our outstanding contribution to the national healthcare system. We are confident that our institute will soon be recognized as a center of excellence in the field of neurosciences.

Sheikh Russel Gastroliver Institute & Hospital is a shining example of patriotism, dedication, and the strong commitment of our honorable Prime Minister, Her Excellency Sheikh Hasina, to elevate healthcare standards to a level that the people of Bangladesh have always dreamed of. This exceptional institute was made possible through her visionary leadership, constant supervision, and guidance.

The Honourable Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Mr. Zahid Maleque MP, played a key role in bringing this project to life. His passion and tireless efforts to overcome numerous challenges were vital in establishing this institution. Thanks to his unwavering support, he has become an integral figure in the fields of Gastroenterology and Hepatology in Bangladesh. His dedication to this noble cause has helped the institute grow, much like nurturing a child. Additionally, the support from all levels of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, the Director General of Health Services, the Ministry of Planning, and the Public Works Department (PWD) deserves special mention for their outstanding contributions to the realization of this project.

Sheikh Russel Gastroliver Institute & Hospital is a 250-bed facility located at Mohakhali TB Gate, Dhaka. It houses both adult and pediatric departments of Medical and Surgical Gastroenterology, along with a state-of-the-art GI Endoscopy suite, a GI physiology lab, OT complex, a 16-bedded ICU, pathology and PCR labs, radiology department, and a transfusion medicine department. The hospital is equipped with the latest medical technology, including the first-ever 3 Tesla MRI with PACS and CT scanners in Bangladesh, setting new benchmarks for quality healthcare in the country.

The hospital also features a modern conference hall with IT facilities, a skill lab, and well-equipped classrooms, making it a truly advanced medical facility. A team of dynamic, highly skilled specialists, blending both young and senior experts from various fields, is dedicated to transforming this institute into a world-class center of excellence.

Our commitment is to significantly contribute to medical science and provide trustworthy, high-quality healthcare services to all. At Sheikh Russel Gastroliver Institute & Hospital, we strive to meet the healthcare needs of every patient with the highest standard of care.

post_title: Sir Salimullah Medical College & Mitford Hospital
post_excerpt: Sir Salimullah Medical College is one of the leading government medical colleges in Bangladesh, located in the historic part of Dhaka, the capital city. Affiliated with the University of Dhaka, it is known for its high standards of medical education…
post_content:
Sir Salimullah Medical College is one of the leading government medical colleges in Bangladesh, located in the historic part of Dhaka, the capital city. Affiliated with the University of Dhaka, it is known for its high standards of medical education and healthcare services. Mitford Hospital, which is part of this college, is the first general hospital in the eastern part of undivided Bengal and remains one of the best medical facilities in Bangladesh.

Mitford Hospital started its journey on May 1, 1858, making it the first hospital of its kind in the region under British rule. The hospital is named after Sir Robert Mitford, the then Collector of Dhaka and a long-serving judge of the Provincial Court of Appeal.

On July 1, 1875, the Dhaka Medical School was established, and on April 2, 1887, the foundation stone for a new school building was laid by W.R. Larminie, the Commissioner of Dhaka Division. The new building was officially opened on September 22, 1889, by Sir S.C. Bayley, the Lieutenant Governor of Bengal.

In 1903, Nawab Sir Salimullah made a generous donation to set up a maternity ward and a female eye ward at the school. Later, in 1962, the Dhaka Medical School became a medical college and was renamed Mitford Medical College. The college was renamed again in 1963 to Sir Salimullah Medical College in honor of Nawab Sir Salimullah’s contributions to the institution.

From 1963 to 1972, the college offered condensed courses for the MBBS degree. In 1972, under the leadership of Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, the college was fully upgraded to a comprehensive medical college. The first batch of MBBS students enrolled in 1973, marking a new chapter in the college’s history.

post_excerpt: Back in 1984, when government medical services had a monopoly in Bangladesh, a group of visionary leaders saw the untapped potential of private medical education. They took it upon themselves to bring about a change and founded the Bangladesh Medical…
post_content:
Back in 1984, when government medical services had a monopoly in Bangladesh, a group of visionary leaders saw the untapped potential of private medical education. They took it upon themselves to bring about a change and founded the Bangladesh Medical Studies Research Institute (BMSRI). Their hard work and dedication led to the creation of Bangladesh Medical College (BMC), the country’s first private medical college, which became a turning point in the history of private medical education in Bangladesh. Since then, BMSRI has continued to play a vital role in opening up new opportunities for healthcare workers and physicians in the private sector. Uttara Adhunik Medical College & Hospital is one of the latest successful institutions to have grown under the umbrella of BMSRI.

With strong leadership, BMSRI ensured the growth of Bangladesh Medical College, which later expanded into the development of a large hospital complex in Uttara, known as Uttara Adhunik Hospital. The hospital first opened its outdoor services on March 12, 2003, and indoor services began on March 10, 2005. Today, the hospital has a capacity of 500 beds and is fully registered with the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Bangladesh.

In 2007, BMSRI took another important step by launching Uttara Adhunik Medical College (UAMC), which opened its doors on January 22, 2008, with 50 students. Over the years, the college has experienced tremendous growth and is now recognized as a top institution for medical education. From the very beginning, UAMC has been affiliated with Dhaka University, further strengthening its reputation as a place of excellence. Both the hospital and medical college take pride in their highly experienced staff and faculty, many of whom have decades of experience in both private and government healthcare sectors. The commitment of the entire team at UAMC&H has made the institution what it is today—a true center of excellence in Bangladesh’s private medical sector, achieving remarkable success in a relatively short time.

The hospital was established with the generous support of a dedicated group of Japanese friends under the Japan Bangladesh Cooperative Society (JBCS), which was formed in Nagoya in March 1995, led by Mr. Shizuo Yamada, the Rotary President of Nagoya.

Key figures from Japan, including Mrs. Etsuko Yamada, Mrs. Yoshiko Matsui, Mrs. Yoko Kobayashi, Mrs. Motoko Fujikawa, Mrs. Kazue Ieda, Mrs. Sakiko Kamata, Mrs. Chikako Kani, Mr. and Mrs. Yokoyama, Mr. and Mrs. Enya, and Mr. Kuroda, played an instrumental role in the formation and ongoing operations of JBCS, as well as the establishment of Aichi Hospital. We are deeply grateful to all of them. Currently, JBCS is led by Mrs. Etsuko Yamada and Mrs. Matsui.

JBCS has become a well-known and respected volunteer organization in both Japan and Bangladesh, providing essential support to Aichi Hospital. The hospital serves thousands of underserved individuals, offering free medical services and treatments. Additionally, more than 200 people have been employed at Aichi Hospital, with their families also benefiting from the services provided.

post_excerpt: The Chittagong Diabetic Association (CDA) is a non-profit, voluntary socio-medical organization and a branch of the Diabetic Association of Bangladesh, Dhaka. It is registered under the Ministry of Social Welfare through the Society’s Registration Act of 1860. Although CDA was…
post_content:
The Chittagong Diabetic Association (CDA) is a non-profit, voluntary socio-medical organization and a branch of the Diabetic Association of Bangladesh, Dhaka. It is registered under the Ministry of Social Welfare through the Society’s Registration Act of 1860. Although CDA was established in 1975 under the guidance of National Professor Dr. Muhammed Ibrahim, with support from a group of dedicated social workers, physicians, and civil servants of Chittagong, it was formally inaugurated on 3rd May 1976 by Prof. Dr. Muhammed Ibrahim, who was then a cabinet member and consultant for Health and Family Planning for the Government of Bangladesh.

Initially, CDA began its temporary operations at the Kapas Gola Municipal Community Center in 1975. Over time, it shifted its services to Chittagong General Hospital, then to Mehedibag, and later to Enayet Bazar. In 1998, the Bangladesh Railway Department allocated 1 acre of land in Khulshi to CDA, enabling it to establish a permanent facility.

Currently, CDA is managed by a 27-member Executive Committee and has grown to become the second-largest diabetic hospital in Bangladesh.

post_excerpt: The parent organization began its journey in 1979 as Chattogram Shishu Hospital, starting from a humble beginning. The hospital was established by a group of social workers in an unfinished, dilapidated government building in Agrabad, with permission from the Ministry…
post_content:
The parent organization began its journey in 1979 as Chattogram Shishu Hospital, starting from a humble beginning. The hospital was established by a group of social workers in an unfinished, dilapidated government building in Agrabad, with permission from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. Initially, it offered small outpatient services with one part-time honorary medical officer from Chittagong Medical College and three honorary pediatric consultants. Over time, the two-story building was fully allocated to the organization, with each floor covering 91,000 sqft.

In 1988, the hospital was completed, and in 1994, maternity services were added, bringing the total bed capacity to 150. The hospital was then renamed “Chattogram Maa-O-Shishu Hospital.”

Today, it has grown into a 650-bed hospital offering a wide range of specialized services, including Pediatrics Medicine, Neonatology, Pediatric Surgery, Pediatric Neurology, General Surgery, Obstetrics & Gynecology, Medicine, and its sub-specialties. The outpatient services include consultations, pharmacy, family planning, mother care, Expanded Program for Immunization (EPI), and Physical Medicine, as well as all types of pediatric care. Other services offered are General Medicine, Orthopedic Surgery, ENT, Ophthalmology, Dentistry, Dermatology, Psychiatry, Urology, Medical Oncology, Radiology, Hematology, Pediatric Hematology, Endocrinology, and a highly specialized Child Development Clinic for retarded and handicapped children and adults.

The average daily attendance in the OPD is around 1500-1600 patients, and emergency medical services are available 24/7 for urgent and semi-urgent cases.

The hospital also has a full-fledged Radiology Department with all types of X-Ray facilities, including 2D and 3D ultrasonography with Color Doppler. The Clinical Pathology Department is well-equipped, covering Hematology, Biochemistry, and Microbiology. A modest Blood Transfusion Center provides services round-the-clock.

post_excerpt: Recognizing the shortage of ophthalmic services in the country and the urgent need for more efforts in this area, a group of ophthalmologists and social workers from Chittagong came together to form a voluntary organization called the ‘Bangladesh National Society…
post_content:
Recognizing the shortage of ophthalmic services in the country and the urgent need for more efforts in this area, a group of ophthalmologists and social workers from Chittagong came together to form a voluntary organization called the ‘Bangladesh National Society for the Blind (BNSB)’ Chittagong. It is a community-based, non-government organization with three main goals: prevention, cure, and rehabilitation of the blind and visually impaired. The society started with a modest fund of just US$100. Prof. Rabiul Husain served as the founder Honorary General Secretary of the society.

Within a year, the organization began its outreach activities by setting up a 40-bed Eye Hospital with an outpatient department in a borrowed space at Anderkilla, Chittagong. The support of the late Mr. Zahur Ahmed Chowdhury, the then Minister of Health & Social Welfare, and the overwhelming backing from the local community were key to establishing this service.

From the very beginning, the organization focused on providing services to rural areas through mobile outreach programs. Using the hospital as a base, it launched intensive mobile eye camps to reach underserved rural populations who had suffered needless blindness for many years.

The first mobile eye camp was held in 1973 at Cox’s Bazar, 150 km south of Chittagong. This initiative drew attention from both domestic and international blind welfare workers. Late Sir John Wilson from the UK and Mrs. Rosi Gollmann, Founder President of Andheri-Hilfe e.V. from Germany, offered their support.

In 1975, the Society launched its School Eye Health Programme with the aim of detecting and treating visual and other ocular defects in school-going children at an early stage. This program received support from Andheri-Hilfe.
address: Foy's Lake Approach Rd, Pahartali, Chattogram, 4202

১৯৬০ সালে ৫০০ শয্যা নিয়ে যাত্রা শুরু করা এই হাসপাতালটি আজ সময়ের সাথে সাথে ১৩১৩ শয্যাবিশিষ্ট একটি আধুনিক হাসপাতালে পরিণত হয়েছে। ২০০৭ সাল থেকে এম.আর.আই, সিটি স্ক্যানসহ বিভিন্ন নতুন চিকিৎসা সুবিধা যেমন নিবিড় পরিচর্যা কেন্দ্র (আই.সি.ইউ), করোনারী কেয়ার ইউনিট (সি.সি.ইউ), নিউনেটালজি, এন্ডোস্কপি ইউনিট, রেডিওথেরাপী এবং কিডনি ডায়ালাইজিং ইউনিট যুক্ত করা হয়েছে।

এছাড়া, ঘরোয়া সহিংসতা, নির্যাতন ও নিপীড়ন মোকাবেলার জন্য হাসপাতালটিতে ওয়ান স্টপ ক্রাইসিস সেন্টার এবং ২৫ শয্যাবিশিষ্ট বার্ন ইউনিট রয়েছে। ২০১২ সালে কার্ডিয়াক সার্জারী ইউনিটও যুক্ত করা হয়। সম্প্রতি, করোনা পরীক্ষার জন্য মাইক্রোবায়োলজি বিভাগে স্থাপিত হয়েছে একটি “করোনা পরীক্ষণ ল্যাব”।
address: 57 K.B. Fazlul Kader Rd, Panchlaish, Chattogram, 4203

post_excerpt: Lions Charitable Eye Hospital, Chittagong, had a modest beginning in 1963 when the late Ln. M.R. Siddique, the Father of Lionism in Bangladesh, established the hospital's OPD in a small tin shed on land leased to the Lions Club of…
post_content:
Lions Charitable Eye Hospital, Chittagong, had a modest beginning in 1963 when the late Ln. M.R. Siddique, the Father of Lionism in Bangladesh, established the hospital’s OPD in a small tin shed on land leased to the Lions Club of Chittagong. His vision was to provide free eye treatment to the underprivileged. In 1965, with contributions from Lions members and philanthropists in Chittagong, the first charitable eye hospital in the Subcontinent was set up in a two-story building known as the “Lions Den.”

To ensure the hospital’s efficient management, the Chittagong Lions Foundation was formed in 1966, with representatives from all Lions Clubs in Chittagong. Over time, the hospital expanded into a 100-bed facility, offering free or subsidized cataract surgery to poor patients, particularly from offshore islands and the remote hill districts of the Chittagong Division.

The hospital has been organizing outreach camps with the help of Lions members, bringing primary eye care to underserved areas. Since 1965, we’ve held 4,448 outreach camps, performed 61,888 surgeries at the hospital, and treated over 2 million patients in these camps. In some camps, up to 3,000 patients have been treated, with around 15% selected for cataract surgery.

Today, with support from Operation Eyesight Universal, LCIF, and local donors, the hospital has become a modern eye care facility. We have a dedicated team of ophthalmologists and the necessary equipment to provide affordable eye treatment and surgeries. Further assistance from LCIF will help us transform the hospital into a Tertiary Eye Hospital and Ophthalmic Institute, addressing the critical need for ophthalmologists and paramedics in the country.

Currently, we perform a range of surgeries including SICS & Phaco, DCR, Pterygium, foreign body removal, DCT, Chalazion, Trabeculectomy, enucleation, and evisceration. We also provide diagnoses for diabetic macular edema and other maculopathies, with the help of our newly acquired OCT machine, which also assists in early glaucoma detection by analyzing the optic nerve head and retinal nerve fiber layers.

In our newly opened pediatric ophthalmology clinic, we have started pediatric cataract surgeries, squint surgeries, and other related procedures. Additionally, we plan to launch a Glaucoma Clinic and a diabetic wing to better serve patients with eye conditions related to diabetes.

Last year, we treated 69,448 patients in the hospital, with 2,516 surgeries performed. In our outreach camps, we treated 68,138 patients, detecting cataracts in 4,590 of them, with 1,879 patients undergoing surgery at our facility.
address: Zakir Hossain Rd, Chattogram, Pahartali, Chattogram, 4000

post_excerpt: For many years, the people of Greater Sylhet have recognized the need for professional education and better healthcare services to support community development. During the British colonial period, local leaders were working hard to establish a medical college in Sylhet…
post_content:
For many years, the people of Greater Sylhet have recognized the need for professional education and better healthcare services to support community development. During the British colonial period, local leaders were working hard to establish a medical college in Sylhet in order to improve the existing healthcare system.

In response to these efforts, the government of Assam agreed to set up a medical college in Sylhet, and construction work for the college and hostel began in 1934. However, due to the influence of certain powerful officials, the project was unexpectedly shifted to Guwahati. In time, the Assam government, under public pressure, announced through an official gazette that a medical school would be established in Sylhet instead of a full medical college. Eventually, the proposed building was repurposed and converted into a hospital in 1936. This hospital later played a key role in serving British and allied troops during the Burma Front in World War II.

In 1948, the hospital was further upgraded and transformed into a Medical School, with appropriate teaching staff and residential accommodation, to produce Licentiate Medical Faculty (LMF) doctors.

In 1962, under pressure from various quarters, the Pakistan government decided to introduce a five-year graduate program leading to the MBBS degree. However, the number of seats available was still far below what was needed for the growing population.

post_excerpt: Bangladesh is quickly emerging as a role model in various aspects of healthcare development, especially among developing countries. However, it is clear that the government alone cannot meet the growing, complex, and diverse healthcare needs of the population. In addition…
post_content:
Bangladesh is quickly emerging as a role model in various aspects of healthcare development, especially among developing countries. However, it is clear that the government alone cannot meet the growing, complex, and diverse healthcare needs of the population. In addition to government medical colleges, private medical colleges in Bangladesh play a vital role in producing skilled healthcare professionals and providing specialized medical services at affordable costs to the people. Location is a key factor in establishing a new medical college, and Sylhet stands out as an ideal choice.

Sylhet is not only known as the religious capital of Bangladesh but is also a popular tourist destination, attracting visitors both nationally and internationally. The city enjoys excellent communication links via road, rail, and air from the capital, Dhaka, and other parts of the country. In addition to its natural beauty and rich religious heritage, Sylhet has evolved into an “Education City” with many prestigious public and private colleges and universities. Among them, Parkview Medical College & Hospital (PMCH) is one of the leading private medical colleges in Bangladesh, contributing significantly to the nation’s healthcare and education sectors.

post_title: Sylhet MAG Osmani Medical College & Hospital
post_excerpt: Sylhet MAG Osmani Medical Hospital is located in the heart of Sylhet division, near the banks of the Surma River in the north-eastern part of Bangladesh. The hospital serves a population of approximately 9 million people in Sylhet division. The…
post_content:
Sylhet MAG Osmani Medical Hospital is located in the heart of Sylhet division, near the banks of the Surma River in the north-eastern part of Bangladesh. The hospital serves a population of approximately 9 million people in Sylhet division. The city of Sylhet, known as the spiritual capital of Bangladesh, is renowned for its scenic beauty, lush tea gardens, and natural attractions. Popular spots like Madhabkunda, the largest natural waterfall in the country, Jaflong, with its stunning natural stone collection sites, and the picturesque tea gardens, draw both tourists and locals alike for relaxation and cultural enjoyment.

Sylhet is also home to significant religious landmarks, such as the shrine of Hazrat Shahjalal (R.) and Hazrat Shahparan (R.), which attract visitors from across the globe. Additionally, the house and temple of the famous Hindu saint, Sri Chitannya Dev, are located near the city, adding to its cultural and religious importance. These natural and spiritual treasures have helped elevate the status of Sylhet and, by extension, Sylhet MAG Osmani Medical College Hospital.

Originally established as an institute hospital in 1936, the hospital became a medical school in 1948, and in 1962 it was upgraded to a full-fledged medical college hospital at its former site. In 1978, the hospital moved to its current location, and in 1986, it was renamed Sylhet MAG Osmani Medical College Hospital in honour of General MAG Osmani, a liberation war hero and the then Chief of Armed Forces, who was born in this region.
address: MAG Usmani Medical College Hospital, Sylhet 3100
