post_excerpt: Labaid Group adds yet another feather in its ornate cap - Labaid Hospital commissioned on 15 July 2004. A new era in the field of care is dawned, a new chapter opened. Labaid Hospital - the fist international standard super-specialty hospital…
post_content:
Labaid Group adds yet another feather in its ornate cap – Labaid Hospital commissioned on 15 July 2004. A new era in the field of care is dawned, a new chapter opened.

Labaid Hospital – the fist international standard super-specialty hospital of the country, dedicated exclusively to the diagnosis and treatment of cardiovascular diseases have been built on a six storied building at, Dhanmondi – a commercial cum residential hub of Dhaka City, covering a floor space of more than 100000 sq. feet.

Equipped with most modern and state-of-the-art equipment and machineries, the Labaid Hospital makes a solemn commitment to the patients of the country – a commitment to maintain mastery and marvel in acre, to be compassionate and respectful to patients’ beliefs and values and to make sound decision in the interest of the patients.

A band of highly skilled, dedicated and experienced surgeons, anaesthesiologists, perfusionists, nurses and technicians garnered from both home and abroad work synergistically and collaboratively under close supervision of renowned specialists of international repute not only provide 24-hour services to the patients but also remain standby to deal with any emergency conditions. Labaid Hospital – a hospital with a difference, strives to ensure that patients receive care in the most appropriate setting – in an environment of privacy, kindness and understanding based on LCH spirit of ‘Care First’ resulting in optimum clinical outcome, greater patient convenience and lower treatment costs. Our approach is comprehensive, physician-led and cost-effective. All our resources are dedicated to keeping the heart healthy and treating it when it is not.
Total Team

The fundamental strength of Labaid Hospital is its two teams – one of cardiologists and other surgery. The teams are composed of highly skilled, experienced and eminent cardiologists, surgeons and anaesthesiologists. Besides, a band of qualified and experienced nurses, technicians and other support personnel garnered from home and abroad are providing constant care to the patients. There exists an unprecedented amount of amity, understanding and cooperation among the members of the teams which are sine-qua-non for successful treatment out come of ailments.

Patient Well Being – A Priority Concern
Patients’ well being and recovery hinge around good nursing care and comfort to be derived from friendly and cheerful services and appropriate surrounding. Labaid Hospital assures its patients unsurpassed level of comfort, nursing care and convenience in an atmosphere of peace, tranquility and understanding. Its customer care service department works full time to ensure that patients return home with positive experience.

Commitment to Highest Standard
The highest standard of internationally accepted operational procedures, infection control protocols and safety guidelines are practices in Labaid Hospital. The hospital enforces zero tolerance in compromising on such vital issues.

Future Plan
Labaid Hospital aims to attain the following:

  1. To turn Labaid Hospital into a ‘Centre of Excellence’ in terms of training, research and development in cardiovascular diseases
  2. To introduce newer treads in care through continued and sustained efforts utilizing cutting edge technology and expertise
  3. To attain leadership status in pediatric surgery
  4. To perform heart transplant surgery

Conclusion
Despite remarkable growth and development that have taken place in health care delivery system in private sector, a staggering number of patients still stream to foreign lands for better treatment 55% of whom are patients. A rough estimate has it that more than US$ 200 million is siphoned off every year from state coffer to foot the bill of medical treatment abroad.

Rapid urbanization, ever changing life style coupled with physical inactivity and unhealthy food habit amongst urban population have contributed to increased incidence of heart ailments in this category of people. Labaid Specialized Hospital, a super specialty hospital of international standard manned by renowned cardiologists, cardiac surgeons and other support staff shall play an all important role in providing optimum care and treatment to cardiac patients of the country.

Hitech Multicare Hospital Ltd., established in November 1995, is a renowned medical centre located in the luxurious and well-facilitated areas of Kafrul and Cantonment, Dhaka. Approved by the local government and governed by a Board of Directors, the hospital is equipped with state-of-the-art medical equipment and staffed by a dedicated team of experienced consultants, technicians, officers, and support staff.

The hospital was founded with a commitment to addressing global health challenges, particularly the threats posed by infectious diseases such as Avian Flu, HIV, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, and Tuberculosis. These diseases remain a leading cause of morbidity, mortality, and productivity loss, especially in developing countries. However, with increased global mobility, these health issues now transcend borders, affecting developed nations and emerging economic giants alike.

Hitech Multicare Hospital Ltd. recognises the critical need for health security and has developed screening and management programs to address these global concerns. These programs cater to the increasing number of job seekers, travellers, officials, and students from Bangladesh moving internationally, helping to mitigate health risks and ensure safety.

By combining modern medical technologies with a professional and highly skilled team, Hitech Multicare Hospital Ltd. continues to provide quality healthcare services while contributing to global health security.

Currently, 76 foreign students are enrolled, comprising 50 male and 16 female students. The college continually evaluates and enhances its programs to ensure the delivery of top-quality medical education. Its educational approach focuses on creating a supportive learning environment, equipping students with the knowledge, skills, and professionalism needed to excel in the medical field.

Green Life Medical College is approved by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MOHFW) and the Bangladesh Medical and Dental Council (BMDC). It is affiliated with the University of Dhaka and recognised by international organisations, including the General Medical Council, United Kingdom. It is also listed in the World Directory of Medical Schools and the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates, further solidifying its reputation as a leading institution for medical education.

post_excerpt: In 1992, Kabir Ahmed Bhuiyan and Md. Saiful Islam envisioned establishing a fully equipped private hospital in Dhaka. To turn their vision into reality, they sought advice from owners of reputed hospitals and eminent doctors in the country. Kabir Ahmed…
post_content:
In 1992, Kabir Ahmed Bhuiyan and Md. Saiful Islam envisioned establishing a fully equipped private hospital in Dhaka. To turn their vision into reality, they sought advice from owners of reputed hospitals and eminent doctors in the country. Kabir Ahmed Bhuiyan also visited hospitals in Singapore, Bangkok, Malaysia, and India, studying their business models, management strategies, administrative techniques, service types, and standards. Based on these insights, they decided to create a modern hospital in Dhaka, offering a complete range of diagnostic facilities and medical treatments under one roof.

This vision materialised in 1993 with the establishment of Comfort Diagnostic Centre (Pvt) Ltd and Comfort Nursing Home (Pvt) Ltd, initially operating from a rented building in Dhanmondi, Dhaka. Comfort quickly gained popularity among patients and doctors due to its superior services and patient-focused care. As demand grew, it became evident that a larger, dedicated facility was needed.

In response, an 11-storey hospital building was constructed in a prime location in Dhaka. In October 1999, Comfort shifted its operations to this new complex, equipped with world-class medical technology to support a modern diagnostic centre and hospital. At that time, Comfort was the first private institution in Bangladesh to provide such comprehensive healthcare services under one roof.

To accommodate even more clients, Comfort later expanded by opening a branch in Uttara Model Town, Dhaka, solidifying its reputation as a leader in private healthcare in Bangladesh.
address: 167/B, Comfort Tower, Green Road, 24, Dhanmondi, Dhaka-1205

post_excerpt: The Diabetic Association of Bangladesh (BADAS) was founded on 28 February 1956 in Dhaka, led by the late National Professor Dr. M Ibrahim (1911-1989), along with a group of social workers, philanthropists, physicians, and civil servants. It began its journey…
post_content:
The Diabetic Association of Bangladesh (BADAS) was founded on 28 February 1956 in Dhaka, led by the late National Professor Dr. M Ibrahim (1911-1989), along with a group of social workers, philanthropists, physicians, and civil servants. It began its journey with a small outpatient clinic in 1957, operating from a modest 380 sq ft semi-permanent structure in Segun Bagicha, Dhaka. Over time, this clinic evolved into the renowned Ibrahim Memorial Diabetes Centre at Shahbagh, Dhaka, named in honour of Prof. M Ibrahim after his passing in 1989.

BADAS is recognised for its unique and comprehensive diabetes care and research program, serving patients nationwide. This program is managed through its central institute, the Bangladesh Institute of Research & Rehabilitation in Diabetes, Endocrine, and Metabolic Disorders (BIRDEM). BIRDEM is a global leader in diabetes care, operating the world’s largest diabetic outpatient facility under one roof and a 650-bed inpatient hospital offering modern multidisciplinary care.

In addition to patient care, BIRDEM is a hub for education and research. It offers 14 postgraduate courses and diplomas, making it the largest private-sector provider of postgraduate medical education in Bangladesh. Recognised by the World Health Organization (WHO) as a Regional Collaborating Centre for Research in the Prevention and Control of Diabetes, BIRDEM is a leader in biomedical research.

Further expanding its services, BADAS operates the Mohila and Shishu Diabetes Endocrine and Metabolic Hospital in Segunbagicha, approved by ECNEC. BIRDEM comprises six outpatient departments, 28 inpatient units, six laboratories, and ten research and academic departments, making it a comprehensive centre for diabetes care, education, and research.

With 12 state-of-the-art operating theaters and more than 30 clinical specialties, United…
post_content:
Established in August 2006 beside the serene Gulshan Lake, United Hospital is one of Bangladesh’s largest private healthcare facilities, spanning 450,000 square feet and accommodating over 500 patients. With 12 state-of-the-art operating theaters and more than 30 clinical specialties, United Hospital offers dedicated ‘Centers of Excellence’ in Cardiac, Cancer, Renal, Surgical, Neuroscience, Mother & Child Health, and Critical Care, led by some of the country’s most respected doctors.

Since opening, United Hospital has achieved significant milestones: conducting over 420,000 cardiac investigations, performing 55,000 cardiac procedures, and completing 14,000 heart surgeries with a success rate of 98%. Our renal services have reached 295,000 hemodialysis sessions and completed 39 kidney transplants, while our cancer care services have provided 185,000 radiation therapy sessions. Additionally, our health checkup program has served 196,000 patients.

United Hospital is a pioneer in advanced medical techniques in Bangladesh. It introduced the Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implant (TAVI) for aortic stenosis, houses the country’s only iGS 520 Hybrid Cardio-Neuro Cathlab, and was the first to receive ISO 15189:2012 accreditation for its pathology lab. The Cancer Care Centre is home to Bangladesh’s only Medical Cyclotron, which produces radioactive isotopes used nationwide, and the first TrueBeam Linear Accelerator for precise, side-effect-minimized radiation therapy. Our Renal Centre leads with Hemo Diafiltration and Bioimpedance technology, and our Radiology & Imaging Centre offers the country’s first 3.0T Signa Pioneer Wide Bore MRI.

Guided by a mission to provide compassionate care, United Hospital is dedicated to earning the trust of our patients through our skilled physicians, nurses, and support teams. We are committed to helping and healing, delivering quality care with empathy and professionalism, and building upon our reputation as a trusted healthcare leader in Bangladesh.

post_excerpt: Labaid is one of the largest and most trusted private healthcare brands in Bangladesh. We’re well-known for our pioneering efforts, like establishing the first super-specialty cardiac hospital in the country. Our centers are now everywhere in Bangladesh, providing high-quality diagnostic…
post_content:
Labaid is one of the largest and most trusted private healthcare brands in Bangladesh. We’re well-known for our pioneering efforts, like establishing the first super-specialty cardiac hospital in the country. Our centers are now everywhere in Bangladesh, providing high-quality diagnostic and consultation services all under one roof. With around 3 million patient visits each year, nearly 8,000 dedicated employees, and about 1,000 top-notch consultant physicians, we’re here to serve you.

Dr. A M Shamim, a visionary physician-entrepreneur, has been leading Labaid for over 35 years. He has played a significant role in transforming private healthcare in Bangladesh. By creating a quality platform for our own physicians and medical practitioners, Labaid has been instrumental in changing public perception. Today, people believe that complex treatments like transplants and cardiovascular surgeries can achieve excellent outcomes right here in Bangladesh with Bangladeshi doctors.

Labaid has also expanded into various sectors to add value to our community. We’ve set up a pharmaceutical plant to provide quality medicines, established educational institutes to address the shortage of skilled healthcare professionals, built modern homes and infrastructure through Labaid Properties, and created Labaid Agros to supply organic and quality foods to our patients and customers.

When you think of Labaid, think of trust. We’re an institution that people can rely on to fulfill their everyday needs and aspirations. We take pride in what we’ve achieved and continuously strive to be a “Home of Trust.”

Our History

Labaid\\’s journey began in 1989. Dr. A M Shamim recalls, “When we started, patients in Dhaka had to wait a week for their results. We wanted to make things easier, so we created a one-stop center for doctors, diagnostics, and pharmacies.” This idea has proven successful, leading to the establishment of 30 Labaid Diagnostic Centers across the country, with more in the pipeline.

For over three decades, Dr. Shamim and his team have focused on finding sustainable ways to improve Bangladesh\\’s healthcare system. “We’re always looking for areas where solutions are needed,” he says. Our pioneering spirit has driven us to diversify our services. In 2004, we opened the Labaid Cardiac Hospital, eliminating the need for many Bangladeshis to seek treatment abroad. This was followed by the Labaid Specialized Hospital in 2006, the first multi-disciplinary super-specialty corporate hospital, and then the Labaid Cancer Hospital. The Labaid Specialized Hospital is a shining example of how state-of-the-art technology and efficient IT systems can work together for better healthcare.

We understand that cutting-edge technology requires skilled staff, which is why we’ve established three educational institutions: the State University of Bangladesh (2002), the Bangladesh Institute of Medical and Dental Technology (1996) – the first of its kind in the country, and the State College of Health Science (2006). These institutes train health professionals, nurses, paramedics, physiotherapists, and dental technicians to meet the growing demand in our medical facilities.

To tackle the issue of counterfeit medicines, Labaid has built its own pharmaceutical manufacturing plant and actively monitors the supply chain. “We want to provide the best possible medicines to our patients, so we invested in a top-notch factory and quality raw materials,” Dr. Shamim explains. We\\’ve also opened 25 Labaid Pharmacies nationwide to ensure safe access to medicines for consumers. Our practices aim to inspire 150,000 pharmacies to maintain high standards.

Labaid Agros, established in 2004, provides fresh and organic food, while Labaid Properties Ltd, founded in 1995, ensures reliable housing and infrastructure for people.

Looking ahead, Dr. Shamim envisions myhealth.com, an Amazon-style platform for the entire healthcare sector. “This will create a network of hospitals, diagnostic centers, and pharmacies for easy access to healthcare services,” he shares.

Our Vision

At Labaid, we believe that excellence through innovation is the core of our vision. Customer satisfaction and lasting relationships are part of our DNA. In a rapidly changing world, we know that only innovation can bring excellence and add value to our products and services.

Our Values

We are dedicated to serving humanity. At Labaid, we work passionately to help people live healthier and happier lives. We recognize our responsibility to the community, as they trust us to meet their needs. We consider it our sacred duty to give back to the land that nurtured us. Our commitment to quality, care, and trust defines us as a company. Our core values are:

  • Integrity
  • Excellence
  • Respect for Individuals

Integrity: We strive for the highest integrity in all our business dealings, believing that honesty, truth, and fairness are essential.

Excellence: We aim for the highest standards in every activity and in the quality of products and services we provide. Our journey towards excellence is ongoing.
